design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate themed planets as Bucky, defeat enemies with projectile combat, progress to boss encounters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming precision combined with enemy avoidance and resource management (ammunition/health)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Space rabbit mercenary fighting alien toads across hostile worlds
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed platformer with above-average design quality (directed by Masato Maegawa, future Treasure founder); treats source material seriously with multi-world progression structure; includes anti-piracy Hard Mode enforcement suggesting designer intent around difficulty tuning

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives with health bar per stage
difficulty.continues_system: Password system for progression continuation across sessions
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; password system allows mid-game restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Password system grants player control over where to continue; anti-piracy Hard Mode (one-hit death) forces extreme difficulty on pirated copies as punishment mechanism rather than optional challenge

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon/power-up pickups within levels (projectile ammunition, health recovery)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Planet-by-planet progression through sequential boss defeats
replayability_hooks: Password system enables stage selection for practice
Multiple difficulty enforcement (Hard Mode via anti-piracy)
Unused content suggests potential for expanded content in development

world_building_techniques: Themed planets (Yellow Planet, Blue Planet, Cell) establish alien worlds
Character-driven protagonist from established cartoon IP
Enemy types reflect planetary hazards and factions

boss_design.philosophy: Planet-culminating encounters with mechanical identity tied to world theme; tank boss on Yellow Planet uses animated wheels suggesting environmental integration
notable_bosses: Yellow Planet Tank Boss (multi-phase, wheel animation suggests state changes)

faction_indicators: Toad Wars framing (toads as antagonist faction)
Salvage Chute enemies (Tribeorg) indicate resource extraction warfare
danger_signals: Electrical zappers in Cell level (controlled environment hazard)
Prison warthogs in Cell (captive creatures, evidence of subjugation)
Progressive world corruption from Yellow to Blue to Cell suggests escalating threat
environmental_storytelling: Cracked ice blocks on Blue Planet suggest environmental damage
Tank presence on Yellow Planet indicates military occupation
Cell-level prison infrastructure shows systematic control
Unused cannon/tank graphics imply expanded militarization concept

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Bucky O'Hare NES development data, unused assets, anti-piracy mechanisms)
